title: Biodiversity Protection: Small Private Steps, Public Sector as the Cornerstone
author: Leogrande, Angelo
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: ISTAT data on investments in Italy for biodiversity and landscape protection between 2016 and 2022 provides a significant overview of how the country has managed, economically, an area that is both delicate and strategically important for environmental balance and quality of life. The analysis involves two main categories of actors: companies as specialized and secondary producers, and public administrations and private social institutions (ISPs). The overall picture is heavily skewed in favor of the public sector, which accounts for nearly all investments, while the contribution from private companies, although growing, remains marginal in absolute terms. Overall, total investment increased from €2,342.8 million in 2016 to €2,497.6 million in 2022, representing an absolute increase of €154.8 million and a percentage change of 6.61%.
